brief_title: R21/Matrix-M Malaria Vaccine in Healthy Young Children in Endemic Areas
official_title: A Phase 2, Randomized, Controlled Study of the R21/Matrix-M Malaria Vaccine Administered as a Three-Dose Primary Series in Healthy Children Aged 5-17 Months in Malaria-Endemic Areas
interventions: R21/Matrix-M vaccine 5μg R21 + 50μg Matrix-M adjuvant IM, 3 doses at 4-week intervals
Rabies vaccine (Verorab) as active comparator, IM, 3 doses at 4-week intervals
brief_summary: This Phase 2 study evaluates the efficacy and safety of the R21/Matrix-M malaria vaccine in healthy children aged 5-17 months in malaria-endemic areas. The primary endpoint is vaccine efficacy against clinical malaria (defined as fever >= 37.5°C plus P. falciparum parasitemia > 5000/μL) over 12 months of follow-up.
raw_inclusion_text:
1. Healthy children aged 5-17 months at first vaccination

2. Residing in the study area with no plans to relocate within 18 months

3. Written informed consent from parent/guardian

4. Up to date on routine EPI vaccinations per local schedule

5. Body weight >= 5 kg and appropriate weight-for-age (z-score >= -3)

6. No acute illness or fever (>= 37.5°C) at time of vaccination

7. Available for the full 18-month follow-up period

8. No history of severe allergic reaction to any vaccine

9. No blood product received within 3 months

10. Parent/guardian able to bring child for scheduled visits
raw_exclusion_text:
1. Current acute malaria infection requiring treatment

2. History of severe malaria

3. Known immunodeficiency (including HIV-exposed uninfected on prophylaxis)

4. Chronic use of immunosuppressive medications

5. Known or suspected hypersensitivity to vaccine components

6. Participation in another interventional vaccine trial

7. Severe malnutrition (weight-for-height z-score < -3)

8. Congenital abnormalities or chronic disease requiring ongoing treatment

9. Receipt of another investigational vaccine or drug within 30 days

inclusion_criteria.text: Healthy children aged 5-17 months at first vaccination
Residing in the study area with no plans to relocate within 18 months
Up to date on routine EPI vaccinations per local schedule
Body weight >= 5 kg and appropriate weight-for-age (z-score >= -3)
No acute illness or fever (>= 37.5°C) at time of vaccination
Available for the full 18-month follow-up period
No history of severe allergic reaction to any vaccine
No blood product received within 3 months
Parent/guardian able to bring child for scheduled visits

exclusion_criteria.text: Current acute malaria infection requiring treatment
Known immunodeficiency (including HIV-exposed uninfected on prophylaxis)
Known or suspected hypersensitivity to vaccine components
Participation in another interventional vaccine trial
Congenital abnormalities or chronic disease requiring ongoing treatment
Receipt of another investigational vaccine or drug within 30 days
